title: rubus × viridiformis
intro: Rubus × viridiformis, also known as the hybrid raspberry, is a plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a cross between the European raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the fragrant raspberry (Rubus fruticosus). The leaves and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus × viridiformis include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Rubus × viridiformis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is also used in herbal infusions and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
